Major A. NJ. Anderson of the Col
vile and Spokane Indian reservations,
is in the city at present.


The county commissioners yester-
day voted $40 a month for six months
for Captain McClelland's Ondawa Inn.


A government civil service eXami-
nation for teachers in the Philippines
is scheduled for this city Novem-

A cottage prayer meeting will be
held tonight by the jefferson street
Methodists at the home of w. +.
Heller. 1898 Dean avenue.


Officers of the Potlatcch Lumber
company say that they are satisfied
with the strike in the Palouse. as
they do not care about logging at
present.


DR.. c. NJ. Baskins of Wilbur is in
the city on professional business TO-
day.


w. Fl. Babcock. Walla Walla
wheat king, Is in Spokane today.


The Club Cafe always in tile lead.
Our 25c merchants lunch with beer
or'wine from 1870 to 2 p m. as good
as ever Headquarters for steaks,
chops. etc. l. Chilberg. manager


County Treasurer Mudgett leaves
tonight to deliver the $4,000 worth
of county bonds issued in refundment
of an equal amount of courthouse and
Jail bonds. His destination is Chi
cago, where he will receive the old
bonds and about $1,000 premium. The
commissioners yesterday appropri-
ated $3.50 for his expenses.


Peter Limacker and Miss Emma r.
Rohrer were married yesterday in
Spokane. going Immediately to their
ranch for wedding dinner


r. NJ. Turner has sold his grocery
business at the corner of Broadway
and Monroe to w. d. Kip.


Harry T. Roberts, aged Is years.
died of pulmonary tuberculosis at his
home, 2203 Mallon avenue, last night,
Funeral services will be held in Grace
Baptist church tomorrow at p. m.


Easy Work Soap-The largest cake
on the market for cents. No fancy
wrapper-all soap.


Rev. NJ. Davis of Harrison, Idaho,
and Miss Nellie +. Pugh were mar
ried at the home of the bride, 1920
College avenue.


+. w. Barrough, an inmate of the
state hospital at Medical Lake, died
yesterday and the remains were ship
ped to today to Ellensburg. He was
10 years of age.


George Weir, the Northern Pacific
auditor who died at Tacoma. will be
buried tomorrow.


Ben Franklin, the philosopher, said:
"a penny saved is a penny earned"
One dollar deposited each week in
the savings bank department of the
Spokane & Eastern Trust company
will in one year amount to $527S;
in five. s2S6.11; in ten, s634SS; in
twenty, S157S33; in thirty, s29SO.21;
in forty, s5D6334 January April.
July and October are the interest
periods.

lister Ludden of the local land Office,
died yesterday afternoon at the fam
lily residence after a long illness Of
consumption. Funeral tomorrow
afternoon from the residence.


If You want 10 nuy or rent sewing
machine, see US. We handle all makes
and don't care which you want. East

1t is understood that there will be
no reduction of the running force
on the Northern Pacific.


No doctor like the right doctor and
no drug store like the right drug store
when you are sick. The Red Cross
Pharmacy is the one. Telephone Main
5l4.


Friday evening, the 18th inst, is
the time set for the Woman's Hospi-
tai corps dance In the dining rooms
of the Hotel Spokane, kindly donated
for the charity by Proprietor Nor
man.


Mr.. and Mrs. A. h. Campbell and
Mr.. and Mrs. Patrick Clark and
daughter have engaged passage on
the Augusta Victoria for February
IN contemplation Of a trip 20 the
Mediterranean.
